skill.name: vision-analysis
skill.description: Analyze images, screenshots, charts, and UI mockups with the MiniMax vision MCP tool. The skill supports description, OCR, UI review, chart extraction, and object identification workflows.
skill.summary: Use MiniMax vision tools to inspect images and extract useful visual information.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but review shows most are false positives from markdown backticks, URLs, API-key placeholders, and mode names in SKILL.md. The confirmed risks are legitimate but important: users are instructed to configure and run an external MiniMax MCP package, provide an API key, and process local or remote images through a third-party service.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Package Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users or agents to configure MiniMax MCP by running the minimax-coding-plan-mcp package through uvx. This is expected for the skill, but it executes third-party code and should require user approval and package verification.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and package name are explicit in the skill text. The context is documented setup, so the risk is real but not evidence of malicious hidden execution.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image Processing and API Key Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill requires MINIMAX_API_KEY and configures MINIMAX_API_HOST for MiniMax API access. Image contents, OCR text, screenshots, and chart data may be sent to a third-party service for analysis.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key and API host configuration are directly shown. The skill purpose requires network vision analysis, but users need a privacy warning for sensitive images and credentials.

medium_findings.title: Local File Access for Image Paths
medium_findings.description: The skill supports local file paths when the MiniMax MCP is configured with file access. This can expose local screenshots or documents to the MCP tool if users provide sensitive paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly mentions configuration files and local file paths. No automatic broad file collection was found, but user-selected files may contain sensitive content.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives from Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Many reported shell, weak cryptography, and blocker findings are markdown backticks, file extensions, headings, mode names, or example output blocks rather than executable code. No evidence of prompt injection, obfuscation, or hidden malicious instructions was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms these sections are documentation tables and prompt examples. The scanner likely treated markdown backticks and ordinary words as code or cryptographic patterns.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and API URLs
low_findings.description: The skill includes MiniMax documentation and API host URLs. These are expected for setup and service use, but they create a dependency on an external provider.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible and point to MiniMax service endpoints or documentation. No evidence was found that they are covert exfiltration endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Setup Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup instructions include commands that install or run an external MCP package. This pattern should be displayed with a warning before use in marketplace clients.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is directly present and relevant to installation. It is disclosed setup behavior, so it does not justify a critical malicious classification.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Configuration Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks users to provide MINIMAX_API_KEY in MCP configuration. This is normal for API-backed tools but requires secure secret handling guidance.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable is explicitly documented. It is a legitimate credential setup pattern rather than evidence that the skill reads or exfiltrates secrets itself.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast visual analysis without writing custom OCR or image prompts. This skill routes image tasks to MiniMax vision workflows for descriptions, text extraction, UI review, chart reading, and object identification.
actual_capabilities: Detects common image file extensions in user messages and selects a vision workflow.
Uses the MiniMax_understand_image MCP tool for image description and visual understanding.
Extracts visible text from screenshots, documents, and other image-based content.
Reviews UI mockups and wireframes with strengths, issues, and improvement suggestions.
Reads charts and graphs to summarize visible labels, data points, and trends.
Identifies visible objects, people, activities, and approximate locations in an image.
limitations: Requires a MiniMax Token Plan subscription and a valid MINIMAX_API_KEY.
Requires the MiniMax MCP tool to be installed and available in the host client.
Image results depend on image clarity, resolution, format support, and model accuracy.
Sensitive images may be processed by a third-party service and need user review first.

use_cases.title: Review Product Screenshots
use_cases.description: Analyze app screenshots or UI mockups to identify usability issues, visible text, and improvement opportunities.

use_cases.title: Extract Text from Images
use_cases.description: Turn screenshot or document images into structured text summaries for tickets, notes, and internal workflows.

use_cases.title: Summarize Visual Data
use_cases.description: Read charts, graphs, and diagrams to capture visible labels, trends, and notable visual evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Describe an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this image and describe the main subject, setting, visible text, objects, colors, and overall composition.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Visible Text
prompt_templates.scenario: OCR from a screenshot or document image
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all readable text from this image. Preserve headings, lists, columns, and line breaks where possible.

prompt_templates.title: Review a UI Mockup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface image. List strengths, usability issues, visual hierarchy concerns, and specific improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this chart image. Capture the title, axes, labels, series, readable values, trends, and any uncertainty.

output_examples.input: A screenshot of a settings page with account controls.
output_examples.output: The analysis identifies the page purpose, visible labels, navigation structure, possible accessibility issues, and suggested layout improvements.

output_examples.input: A photo of a printed document with several paragraphs.
output_examples.output: The result extracts the readable text, keeps the paragraph order, and notes any words that are unclear.

output_examples.input: A line chart showing monthly revenue trends.
output_examples.output: The response summarizes the chart title, axes, visible data movement, peak periods, and values that can be read confidently.

best_practices: Ask for one clear analysis mode, such as OCR, UI review, chart extraction, or object detection.
Use high-resolution images with readable text and minimal cropping for better results.
Remove sensitive personal, financial, or confidential content before sending images to the service.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for private images unless third-party processing is acceptable.
Do not assume extracted chart values are exact when labels or markers are unclear.
Do not paste real API keys into shared prompts, screenshots, logs, or public workspaces.

faq.question: Does this skill require a MiniMax account?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires a MiniMax Token Plan subscription and a valid MINIMAX_API_KEY.

faq.question: Can it analyze local image files?
faq.answer: Yes, if the MiniMax MCP server is configured with access to the local file path.

faq.question: Which image tasks does it support?
faq.answer: It supports image description, OCR, UI review, chart data extraction, and object identification.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential screenshots?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow can send image content to MiniMax for processing.

faq.question: Does it install software automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides setup commands for the MiniMax MCP package. Users should approve and verify them before running.

faq.question: Does it guarantee exact OCR or chart values?
faq.answer: No. Accuracy depends on image quality, text size, chart clarity, and model interpretation.
